sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Welcome to the Round 32 clash between 1. FC Union Berlin and SV Werder Bremen at the iconic An der alten Försterei stadium in Berlin! Union Berlin, currently sitting at 13th in the league with 37 points, are desperate for a positive result to climb up the standings. On the other hand, Werder Bremen, positioned at 8th with 47 points, aim to solidify their mid-table status with a win. Both teams come into this match with unbeaten streaks, Union Berlin with no losses in their last 7 matches and Werder Bremen maintaining an unbeaten run over their last 5 games. This sets the stage for an intriguing battle where momentum could play a key role.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Werder Bremen have had the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Union Berlin’s 2. Their most recent encounter on December 21, 2024, saw Werder Bremen dominate with a 4-1 victory at home, where Derrick Köhn earned the Player of the Match title with an impressive 8.1 Sofascore rating. Interestingly, Union Berlin have struggled to keep a clean sheet against Werder Bremen, conceding in their last 3 head-to-head games. Historically, their matches have often seen high corner counts, though only 5 of the last 7 games went over 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Union Berlin are in a curious phase, unbeaten in their last 7 matches but also without a win in their last 3, indicating a string of draws or close encounters. Werder Bremen, meanwhile, are enjoying a 5-match unbeaten run, showing consistency in their performances. Notably, Union Berlin have seen both teams scoring in 5 of their last 7 games, suggesting defensive vulnerabilities. Werder Bremen have had disciplinary issues, with over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 away games, while Union Berlin tend to keep things calmer, with under 4.5 cards in 8 of their last 10 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Union Berlin
sections.text: For Union Berlin, forward Benedict Hollerbach stands out with 8 goals this season, overperforming his expected goals of 5.84 by a significant margin of 2.16. Defender Leopold Querfeld, ranked 1st in the league for the percentage of long balls in total passes at 11.4%, has been in fine form recently, averaging a 7.5 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 1 goal and 2 assists. Andrej Ilić, another forward, has chipped in with 5 goals, showing promise despite a modest rating of 6.87. Midfielder Christopher Trimmel also deserves a mention for his creativity, contributing 3 assists with a solid rating of 6.96.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Werder Bremen
sections.text: Werder Bremen’s attack is led by Jens Stage, a midfielder who has scored 9 goals and provided 5 assists, overperforming his expected goals of 6.47 by 2.53. Forward Marvin Ducksch, a standout in past matchups with an average Sofascore rating of 7.5 against Union Berlin, has tallied 7 goals and 7 assists this season, though he’s underperformed his expected goals of 9.3. Midfielder Romano Schmid, ranked 4th in the league for key passes with 62, is another danger man with a rating of 7.19. Defender Anthony Jung, ranked 4th for ground duels won at 70.9%, adds defensive solidity with a rating of 6.77.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Union Berlin’s season stats reveal a mixed bag, with only 33 goals scored (ranked 15th) but a commendable overperformance in goals versus expected goals, ranking 4th with a difference of 0.62. Their defensive clearances per game are impressive at 30.23, ranked 2nd in the league, and they excel in interceptions at 9.39 per game, ranked 3rd. However, their possession game is poor at 39.29%, ranked 18th, which could be exploited by Werder Bremen. Werder Bremen’s goal distribution shows strength early and late in games, with 23.21% of their goals coming between minutes 16-30 and 19.64% between 76-90. Union Berlin, conversely, have scored 21.74% of their goals in the first 15 minutes, suggesting an early push might be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Discipline and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: With referee Sören Storks officiating, who averages 4.1 yellow cards per game and 0.22 red cards, expect a match with moderate disciplinary action. Union Berlin have accumulated 68 yellow cards this season, averaging 2.19 per game, while Werder Bremen’s recent trend of over 4.5 cards in away matches raises the likelihood of bookings. On set-pieces, Union Berlin average 4.65 corners per game (ranked 12th), and both teams have a recent streak of under 10.5 corners in most matches, with Union Berlin at 4 of their last 5 and Werder Bremen at 5 of their last 6.
